key_insight: ML-driven molecular dynamics shows that MgH2 dehydrogenation is governed by subsurface H2 formation and that Ni-like dopants optimize release by balancing H attraction and H-H coupling.
engineering_relevance: The study offers a quantitative design rule for selecting catalyst dopants and surface orientations to improve hydrogen storage material performance.
method: ML potential-driven MD simulations of pristine and doped MgH2
Surface screening of low-index facets to identify the most active (100) surface
Descriptor analysis across 22 dopants to link electronic structure to hydrogen release
evidence_signal: The abstract reports systematic atomistic screening and machine-learning analysis across surface orientations and 22 dopants, yielding a volcano trend with an optimal Miedema electron-density window.
watch_item: The conclusions depend on simulation-trained potentials and descriptor correlations, so transferability to real microstructures, temperatures, and long-timescale experimental conditions should be validated.
paper_title: Machine Learning Potential-Driven Molecular Dynamics Simulations of Dehydrogenation in Pristine and Doped MgH$_2$
